AAPL.shape_notes: V2: Added bite indentation on right side (rows 4,6 have gap at col 8) and offset leaf/stem (row 0 col 6, row 1 col 5). Bottom split retained. The bite is the key identifier — now present. Leaf is subtle at this resolution but reads correctly. Could still improve: bite could be slightly deeper, body curves could be smoother.
AAPL.color_notes: Using #A3AAAE (silver-gray). Solid contextual choice for Apple's monochrome identity.

history.notes: Basic apple shape, no bite or leaf
Added bite on right side + offset leaf

TSLA.shape_notes: V2: Wide curved crown tapering to pointed stem. Shoulder gap creates characteristic notch. Could improve: more convex crown arch, wider inner gap.

history.notes: Redesigned as emblem silhouette with crown + stem

GOOGL.shape_notes: Open circle G with horizontal bar from right. Opening at top-right reads correctly. Bar entering from right is the key differentiator. Could improve: thinner bar, wider opening gap.
GOOGL.color_notes: Using #4285F4 (Google Blue). Exact primary blue match. Real G uses 4 colors — single-color is a valid simplification.

AMZN.shape_notes: V5: Concave bowl/smile — both ends high, center dips to bottom. Symmetric U-curve spanning full grid width. Arrow tip kicks up-right from the right end with a small barb. Top half empty (smile sits below wordmark). This is the correct Amazon smile geometry: a sideways parenthesis bowing downward. Could improve: slightly asymmetric (left end a touch higher than right, per real logo), smoother curve at larger grid sizes.
AMZN.color_notes: Using #FF9900 (Amazon Orange). Exact match for the smile/arrow color.

history.notes: Letter A with underline — didn't match brand
Diagonal line, not a smile shape
Still too diagonal, not bowing downward
Dropped left-to-right, still not a U-shape
Correct concave bowl smile with arrow tip — user confirmed shape

MSFT.shape_notes: Four-pane window grid is nearly perfect. Equal quadrants, centered single-dot gap divider. Strongest shape match. Could improve: add 4-color quadrants.
MSFT.color_notes: Using #00A4EF (one of four Windows pane colors). Could use 4 colors for the 4 quadrants.

META.shape_notes: V2: Wider infinity loops with clearer center crossing. The two loops are more distinct — left loop (cols 0-4) and right loop (cols 6-10) connected by crossing at col 5. Vertical symmetry improved. The thick bases (row 7) and thinning at top give the lemniscate feel. Could improve: the loops could be rounder, the crossing point could have more visual weight.
META.color_notes: Using #0081FB (Meta blue). Exact match. Real logo has gradient which can't be represented.

history.notes: Infinity shape was too angular and hard to read

NVDA.shape_notes: V2: Asymmetric eye shape — pointed left corner tapering to single dot (row 5), straight vertical right edge (cols 9-10). Hollow center reads as the eye opening. Top and bottom lids curve symmetrically. The asymmetry (pointy left, flat right) matches NVIDIA's actual mark orientation. Could improve: add subtle interior detail for the pupil/swirl.

history.notes: Diagonal parallelogram, didn't read as eye
Asymmetric eye with pointed left, flat right edge

NFLX.shape_notes: Netflix ribbon-N is very well executed. Two vertical bars with clean diagonal slash. Proportions are good. Could improve: slightly thinner diagonal for more ribbon feel.

SPY.shape_notes: V3: Thin-stroke S with rotational symmetry. 2-dot wide strokes throughout. Top cap and bottom cap mirror. Upper-left arm flows diagonally into center bar, center bar flows into lower-right arm. Clean S-curve with proper proportions and no chunkiness. Could improve: subtle weight variation (thicker at terminals).
SPY.color_notes: Using #1B365D (deep navy). Reasonable for State Street/SPDR.

history.notes: Balanced S with better loop proportions
Thin-stroke S with rotational symmetry, proper proportions

improvement_queue.suggestion: Rounder loops, more visual weight at crossing
Thinner horizontal bar, wider gap opening
Slight left-high asymmetry, smoother at larger grids
